steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage provides specific numbers: a 3,500 kg pygmy right whale can eat 1,050 kg of krill per day, while a 66,000 kg bowhead whale can eat 19,800 kg per day. The question asks what can most reasonably be concluded from the text — the answer must follow directly from these stated facts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'As krill consumption by pygmy right whales has decreased, the krill consumption of bowhead whales has increased.': the passage describes no change in consumption over time for either species — this is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The bowhead whale is able to eat more krill per day than the pygmy right whale is.': directly supported — 19,800 kg (bowhead) is more than 1,050 kg (pygmy right whale) per day. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Both pygmy right and bowhead whales can eat krill, but of the two only the pygmy right whale can also subsist on phytoplankton.': the passage never states either species eats phytoplankton — phytoplankton sustains krill, not the whales directly. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Populations of both pygmy right and bowhead whales have declined significantly over the last century due to decreasing levels of iron in the ocean.': the passage says whale populations declined and iron cycling is important, but does not attribute the decline to iron levels. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
